expectations.text: [holdout_regression][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: promotion/finalization behavior remains stable on another feature
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the holdout regression focus and discusses promotion/finalization: "focusing on whether promotion/finalization behavior remains stable on another feature" and explains Phase 7 promotion/finalization contract details (archive existing final, promote draft, write finalization record). It concludes the case cannot be validated due to missing Phase 7 artifacts, but the focus is explicitly covered.

expectations.text: [holdout_regression][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase holdout
expectations.evidence: result.md declares "Phase alignment: Holdout" and frames the assessment as a holdout checkpoint evaluation. Both result.md and execution_notes.md treat missing holdout execution artifacts as a blocking condition: "BLOCKED — insufficient holdout evidence to verify..." This aligns the output with holdout-phase expectations (i.e., evaluating/confirming holdout evidence, and blocking when absent).
